sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.106 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: A first order formula$(x)$in the group language with one free variable is said to be in a class$$of groups if for every group$G$in$$such that the set$G_$of elements in$G$satisfying$$is finite, the subgroup$(G)$generated by$G_$is also finite (cf. 21.105 and Archive 2.45). Is every formula with one free variable concise in the class of residually finite groups? { M.\,Petschick}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
